judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all four expectations with essentially identical, well-grounded findings; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence:
`config/scoring_config.yaml` still contains:
```
expected_label_field: label
```
but commit `53551e8` renamed the gold-label key in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label`... `gold = record.get(gold_field)                 # every record now only has "gold_label", so gold is always None`

report_2.evidence: `config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`. But the eval fixture `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` stores gold labels under the key `"gold_label"`... `record.get(gold_field)` — i.e. `record.get("label")` — returns `None` for every single record... The fix is a one-line change to **`config/scoring_config.yaml`**

report_1.evidence: The only commit dated 2026-06-17 is `53551e8 chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)`, whose diff (`git show 53551e8`) shows the eval-set key rename plus the `REQUIRED_KEYS` update in `score_batch.py`, but no corresponding update to `config/scoring_config.yaml`.

report_2.evidence: This lines up exactly with commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", dated 2026-06-17), which renamed the label key from `"label"` to `"gold_label"` in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`... but forgot the corresponding update to `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_1.evidence: It re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`, which calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` directly and never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`... and never compares against any gold label at all... To correctly reproduce the drop, you need to exercise the actual gold-label comparison the nightly job uses, i.e. run `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py`, which the earlier note used to "re-run the failing tickets individually," never performs any scoring or comparison against gold labels at all... To correctly reproduce the drop, you have to exercise the actual comparison logic in `score_batch.py`

report_1.evidence: Rolling back "the model" would do nothing to `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s stale `expected_label_field`, so the score would not recover... I confirmed this by running `python3 pipeline/score_batch.py`, which printed `accuracy=0.00` on the current HEAD.

report_2.evidence: the bug is entirely in the gold-label field lookup, upstream of any prediction. Rolling back the model would leave `expected_label_field: label` unchanged, `record.get("label")` would still return `None` for every record, and accuracy would still read 0.0 the next night.
